block.description:
## Setting up supporting infrastructure


The example below creates the supporting infrastructure using the STACKIT Terraform provider, including the automatic creation of a TLS certificate resource.

disable_target_security_group_assignment.description: Disable target security group assignemt to allow targets outside of the given network. Connectivity to targets need to be ensured by the customer, including routing and Security Groups (targetSecurityGroup can be assigned). Not changeable after creation.

description.description: The error description contains additional helpful user information to fix the error state of the Application Load Balancer. For example the IP 45.135.247.139 does not exist in the project, then the description will report: Floating IP "45.135.247.139" could not be found.

type.description: The error type specifies which part of the Application Load Balancer encountered the error. I.e. the API will not check if a provided public IP is actually available in the project. Instead the Application Load Balancer with try to use the provided IP and if not available reports TYPE_FIP_NOT_CONFIGURED error. Possible values are: `TYPE_UNSPECIFIED`, `TYPE_INTERNAL`, `TYPE_QUOTA_SECGROUP_EXCEEDED`, `TYPE_QUOTA_SECGROUPRULE_EXCEEDED`, `TYPE_PORT_NOT_CONFIGURED`, `TYPE_FIP_NOT_CONFIGURED`, `TYPE_TARGET_NOT_ACTIVE`, `TYPE_METRICS_MISCONFIGURED`, `TYPE_LOGS_MISCONFIGURED`.

errors.description: Reports all errors a Application Load Balancer has.

external_address.description: The external IP address where this Application Load Balancer is exposed. Not changeable after creation.

id.description: Terraform's internal resource ID. It is structured as `project_id`,`region`,`name`.

labels.description: Labels represent user-defined metadata as key-value pairs. Label count cannot exceed 64 per ALB.

host.description: Hostname to match. Supports wildcards (e.g. *.example.com).

name.description: The name of the cookie to use.

ttl.description: TTL specifies the time-to-live for the cookie. The default value is 0s, and it acts as a session cookie, expiring when the client session ends.

exact_match.description: Exact match for the header value.

name.description: Header name.

exact_match.description: Exact path match. Only a request path exactly equal to the value will match, e.g. '/foo' matches only '/foo', not '/foo/bar' or '/foobar'.

prefix.description: Prefix path match. Only matches on full segment boundaries, e.g. '/foo' matches '/foo' and '/foo/bar' but NOT '/foobar'.

path.description: Routing via path.

exact_match.description: Exact match for the query parameters value.

name.description: Query parameter name.

target_pool.description: Reference target pool by target pool name.

web_socket.description: If enabled, when client sends an HTTP request with and Upgrade header, indicating the desire to establish a Websocket connection, if backend server supports WebSocket, it responds with HTTP 101 status code, switching protocols from HTTP to WebSocket. Hence the client and the server can exchange data in real-time using one long-lived TCP connection.

rules.description: Routing rules under the specified host, matched by path prefix.

hosts.description: Defines routing rules grouped by hostname.

https.description: Configuration for handling HTTPS traffic on this listener.

name.description: Unique name for the listener

port.description: Port number on which the listener receives incoming traffic.

protocol.description: Protocol is the highest network protocol we understand to load balance. Possible values are: `PROTOCOL_UNSPECIFIED`, `PROTOCOL_HTTP`, `PROTOCOL_HTTPS`.

waf_config_name.description: Enable Web Application Firewall (WAF), referenced by name. See "Application Load Balancer - Web Application Firewall API" for more information.

listeners.description: List of all listeners which will accept traffic. Limited to 20.

id.description: ID of the security Group

name.description: Name of the security Group

load_balancer_security_group.description: Security Group permitting network traffic from the LoadBalancer to the targets. Useful when disableTargetSecurityGroupAssignment=true to manually assign target security groups to targets.

name.description: Application Load balancer name.

network_id.description: STACKIT network ID the Application Load Balancer and/or targets are in.

role.description: The role defines how the Application Load Balancer is using the network. Possible values are: `ROLE_UNSPECIFIED`, `ROLE_LISTENERS_AND_TARGETS`, `ROLE_LISTENERS`, `ROLE_TARGETS`.

networks.description: List of networks that listeners and targets reside in.

allowed_source_ranges.description: Application Load Balancer is accessible only from an IP address in this range.

access_control.description: Use this option to limit the IP ranges that can use the Application Load Balancer.

ephemeral_address.description: This option automates the handling of the external IP address for an Application Load Balancer. If set to true a new IP address will be automatically created. It will also be automatically deleted when the Load Balancer is deleted.

credentials_ref.description: Credentials reference for logging. This reference is created via the observability create endpoint and the credential needs to contain the basic auth username and password for the logging solution the push URL points to. Then this enables monitoring via remote write for the Application Load Balancer.

push_url.description: Credentials reference for logging. This reference is created via the observability create endpoint and the credential needs to contain the basic auth username and password for the logging solution the push URL points to. Then this enables monitoring via remote write for the Application Load Balancer.

credentials_ref.description: Credentials reference for metrics. This reference is created via the observability create endpoint and the credential needs to contain the basic auth username and password for the metrics solution the push URL points to. Then this enables monitoring via remote write for the Application Load Balancer.

push_url.description: Credentials reference for metrics. This reference is created via the observability create endpoint and the credential needs to contain the basic auth username and password for the metrics solution the push URL points to. Then this enables monitoring via remote write for the Application Load Balancer.

observability.description: We offer Load Balancer observability via STACKIT Observability or external solutions.

private_network_only.description: Application Load Balancer is accessible only via a private network ip address. Not changeable after creation.

options.description: Defines any optional functionality you want to have enabled on your Application Load Balancer.

plan_id.description: Service Plan configures the size of the Application Load Balancer e.g. 'p10'. See available plans via STACKIT CLI 'stackit beta alb plans' or API https://docs.api.stackit.cloud/documentation/alb/version/v2#tag/Project/operation/APIService_ListPlans

project_id.description: STACKIT project ID to which the Application Load Balancer is associated.

region.description: The resource region (e.g. eu01). If not defined, the provider region is used.

healthy_threshold.description: Healthy threshold of the health checking.

ok_status.description: List of HTTP status codes that indicate a healthy response.

path.description: Path to send the health check request to.

http_health_checks.description: Options for the HTTP health checking.

interval.description: Interval duration of health checking in seconds.

interval_jitter.description: Interval duration threshold of the health checking in seconds.

timeout.description: Active health checking timeout duration in seconds.

unhealthy_threshold.description: Unhealthy threshold of the health checking.

name.description: Target pool name.

target_port.description: The number identifying the port where each target listens for traffic.

ip.description: Private target IP, which must by unique within a target pool.

targets.description: List of all targets which will be used in the pool. Limited to 250.

custom_ca.description: Specifies a custom Certificate Authority (CA). When provided, the target pool will trust certificates signed by this CA, in addition to any system-trusted CAs. This is useful for scenarios where the target pool needs to communicate with servers using self-signed or internally-issued certificates. Enabled needs to be set to true and skip validation to false for this option.

enabled.description: Enable TLS (Transport Layer Security) bridging for the connection between Application Load Balancer and targets in this pool. When enabled, public CAs are trusted. Can be used in tandem with the options either custom CA or skip validation or alone.

skip_certificate_validation.description: Bypass certificate validation for TLS bridging in this target pool. This option is insecure and can only be used with public CAs by setting enabled true. Meant to be used for testing purposes only!

target_pools.description: List of all target pools which will be used in the Application Load Balancer. Limited to 20.

id.description: ID of the security Group

name.description: Name of the security Group

target_security_group.description: Security Group that allows the targets to receive traffic from the LoadBalancer. Useful when disableTargetSecurityGroupAssignment=true to manually assign target security groups to targets.

version.description: Application Load Balancer resource version. Used for concurrency safe updates.

The example below creates the supporting infrastructure using the STACKIT Terraform provider, including the network, network interface, a public IP address and server resources.

algorithm.description: The encryption algorithm that the key will use to encrypt data. Possible values are: `aes_256_gcm`, `rsa_2048_oaep_sha256`, `rsa_3072_oaep_sha256`, `rsa_4096_oaep_sha256`, `rsa_4096_oaep_sha512`, `hmac_sha256`, `hmac_sha384`, `hmac_sha512`, `ecdsa_p256_sha256`, `ecdsa_p384_sha384`, `ecdsa_p521_sha512`.

keyring_id.description: The ID of the associated keyring

project_id.description: STACKIT project ID to which the key is associated.

protection.description: The underlying system that is responsible for protecting the key material. Possible values are: `software`.

purpose.description: The purpose for which the key will be used. Possible values are: `symmetric_encrypt_decrypt`, `asymmetric_encrypt_decrypt`, `message_authentication_code`, `asymmetric_sign_verify`.

region.description: The resource region. If not defined, the provider region is used.

block.description:
KMS Key resource schema. Uses the `default_region` specified in the provider configuration as a fallback in case no `region` is defined on resource level.

 ~> Keys will **not** be instantly destroyed by terraform during a `terraform destroy`. They will just be scheduled for deletion via the API and thrown out of the Terraform state afterwards. **This way we can ensure no key setups are deleted by accident and it gives you the option to recover your keys within the grace period.**

display_name.description: The display name to distinguish multiple keyrings.

KMS Keyring resource schema. Uses the `default_region` specified in the provider configuration as a fallback in case no `region` is defined on resource level.

 ~> Keyrings will **not** be destroyed by terraform during a `terraform destroy`. They will just be thrown out of the Terraform state and not deleted on API side. **This way we can ensure no keyring setups are deleted by accident and it gives you the option to recover your keys within the grace period.**

access_scope.description: The access scope of the key. Default is `PUBLIC`. Possible values are: `PUBLIC`, `SNA`.

Possible values are: `rsa_2048_oaep_sha256`, `rsa_3072_oaep_sha256`, `rsa_4096_oaep_sha256`, `rsa_4096_oaep_sha512`, `rsa_2048_oaep_sha256_aes_256_key_wrap`, `rsa_3072_oaep_sha256_aes_256_key_wrap`, `rsa_4096_oaep_sha256_aes_256_key_wrap`, `rsa_4096_oaep_sha512_aes_256_key_wrap`.

created_at.description: The date and time the creation of the wrapping key was triggered.

description.description: A user chosen description to distinguish multiple wrapping keys.

display_name.description: The display name to distinguish multiple wrapping keys.

expires_at.description: The date and time the wrapping key will expire.

keyring_id.description: The ID of the associated keyring

project_id.description: STACKIT project ID to which the keyring is associated.

protection.description: The underlying system that is responsible for protecting the key material. Possible values are: `software`.

public_key.description: The public key of the wrapping key.

purpose.description: The purpose for which the key will be used. Possible values are: `wrap_symmetric_key`, `wrap_asymmetric_key`.

wrapping_key_id.description: The ID of the wrapping key

disable_security_group_assignment.description: If set to true, this will disable the automatic assignment of a security group to the load balancer's targets. This option is primarily used to allow targets that are not within the load balancer's own network or SNA (STACKIT network area). When this is enabled, you are fully responsible for ensuring network connectivity to the targets, including managing all routing and security group rules manually. This setting cannot be changed after the load balancer is created.

external_address.description: External Load Balancer IP address where this Load Balancer is exposed.

id.description: Terraform's internal resource ID. It is structured as "`project_id`","region","`name`".

port.description: Port number where we listen for traffic.

protocol.description: Protocol is the highest network protocol we understand to load balance. Possible values are: `PROTOCOL_UNSPECIFIED`, `PROTOCOL_TCP`, `PROTOCOL_UDP`, `PROTOCOL_TCP_PROXY`, `PROTOCOL_TLS_PASSTHROUGH`.

name.description: A domain name to match in order to pass TLS traffic to the target pool in the current listener

server_name_indicators.description: A list of domain names to match in order to pass TLS traffic to the target pool in the current listener

target_pool.description: Reference target pool by target pool name.

idle_timeout.description: Time after which an idle connection is closed. The default value is set to 300 seconds, and the maximum value is 3600 seconds. The format is a duration and the unit must be seconds. Example: 30s

tcp.description: Options that are specific to the TCP protocol.

idle_timeout.description: Time after which an idle session is closed. The default value is set to 1 minute, and the maximum value is 2 minutes. The format is a duration and the unit must be seconds. Example: 30s

udp.description: Options that are specific to the UDP protocol.

listeners.description: List of all listeners which will accept traffic. Limited to 20.

name.description: Load balancer name.

role.description: The role defines how the load balancer is using the network. Possible values are: `ROLE_UNSPECIFIED`, `ROLE_LISTENERS_AND_TARGETS`, `ROLE_LISTENERS`, `ROLE_TARGETS`.

networks.description: List of networks that listeners and targets reside in.

acl.description: Load Balancer is accessible only from an IP address in this range.

credentials_ref.description: Credentials reference for logs. Not changeable after creation.

push_url.description: The ARGUS/Loki remote write Push URL to ship the logs to. Not changeable after creation.

logs.description: Observability logs configuration. Not changeable after creation.

credentials_ref.description: Credentials reference for metrics. Not changeable after creation.

push_url.description: The ARGUS/Prometheus remote write Push URL to ship the metrics to. Not changeable after creation.

metrics.description: Observability metrics configuration. Not changeable after creation.

observability.description: We offer Load Balancer metrics observability via ARGUS or external solutions. Not changeable after creation.

private_network_only.description: If true, Load Balancer is accessible only via a private network IP address.

options.description: Defines any optional functionality you want to have enabled on your load balancer.

plan_id.description: The service plan ID. If not defined, the default service plan is `p10`. Possible values are: `p10`, `p50`, `p250`, `p750`.

private_address.description: Transient private Load Balancer IP address. It can change any time.

project_id.description: STACKIT project ID to which the Load Balancer is associated.

region.description: The resource region. If not defined, the provider region is used.

security_group_id.description: The ID of the egress security group assigned to the Load Balancer's internal machines. This ID is essential for allowing traffic from the Load Balancer to targets in different networks or STACKIT network areas (SNA). To enable this, create a security group rule for your target VMs and set the `remote_security_group_id` of that rule to this value. This is typically used when `disable_security_group_assignment` is set to `true`.

healthy_threshold.description: Healthy threshold of the health checking.

interval.description: Interval duration of health checking in seconds.

interval_jitter.description: Interval duration threshold of the health checking in seconds.

timeout.description: Active health checking timeout duration in seconds.

unhealthy_threshold.description: Unhealthy threshold of the health checking.

name.description: Target pool name.

use_source_ip_address.description: If true then all connections from one source IP address are redirected to the same target. This setting changes the load balancing algorithm to Maglev.

session_persistence.description: Here you can setup various session persistence options, so far only "`use_source_ip_address`" is supported.

target_port.description: Identical port number where each target listens for traffic.

ip.description: Target IP

targets.description: List of all targets which will be used in the pool. Limited to 1000.

target_pools.description: List of all target pools which will be used in the Load Balancer. Limited to 20.

version.description: Load balancer resource version. This is needed to have concurrency safe updates.

block.description:
## Setting up supporting infrastructure


The example below creates the supporting infrastructure using the STACKIT Terraform provider, including the network, network interface, a public IP address and server resources.

block.description:
Network resource schema. Must have a `region` specified in the provider configuration.
~> Behavior of not configured `ipv4_nameservers` has changed. When `ipv4_nameservers` is not set, it will be set to the network area's `default_nameservers`.
To prevent any nameserver configuration, the `ipv4_nameservers` attribute should be explicitly set to an empty list `[]`.
In cases where `ipv4_nameservers` are defined within the resource, the existing behavior will remain unchanged.

for.description: Alerts are considered firing once they have been returned for this long. Alerts which have not yet fired for long enough are considered pending. Default is 0s

resolve_timeout.description: The default value used by alertmanager if the alert does not include EndsAt. After this time passes, it can declare the alert as resolved if it has not been updated. This has no impact on alerts from Prometheus, as they always include EndsAt.

smtp_auth_identity.description: SMTP authentication information. Must be a valid email address

smtp_auth_password.description: SMTP Auth using LOGIN and PLAIN.

smtp_auth_username.description: SMTP Auth using CRAM-MD5, LOGIN and PLAIN. If empty, Alertmanager doesn't authenticate to the SMTP server.

smtp_from.description: The default SMTP From header field. Must be a valid email address

smtp_smart_host.description: The default SMTP smarthost used for sending emails, including port number in format `host:port` (eg. `smtp.example.com:587`). Port number usually is 25, or 587 for SMTP over TLS (sometimes referred to as STARTTLS).

global.description: Global configuration for the alerts. If nothing passed the default argus config will be used. It is only possible to update the entire global part, not individual attributes.

auth_identity.description: SMTP authentication information. Must be a valid email address

from.description: The sender email address. Must be a valid email address

send_resolved.description: Whether to notify about resolved alerts.

smart_host.description: The SMTP host through which emails are sent.

to.description: The email address to send notifications to. Must be a valid email address

name.description: Name of the receiver.

api_url.description: The host to send OpsGenie API requests to. Must be a valid URL

priority.description: Priority of the alert. Possible values are: `P1`, `P2`, `P3`, `P4`, `P5`.

send_resolved.description: Whether to notify about resolved alerts.

tags.description: Comma separated list of tags attached to the notifications.

google_chat.description: Google Chat webhooks require special handling, set this to true if the webhook is for Google Chat.

ms_teams.description: Microsoft Teams webhooks require special handling, set this to true if the webhook is for Microsoft Teams.

send_resolved.description: Whether to notify about resolved alerts.

url.description: The endpoint to send HTTP POST requests to. Must be a valid URL

continue.description: Whether an alert should continue matching subsequent sibling nodes.

group_by.description: The labels by which incoming alerts are grouped together. For example, multiple alerts coming in for cluster=A and alertname=LatencyHigh would be batched into a single group. To aggregate by all possible labels use the special value '...' as the sole label name, for example: group_by: ['...']. This effectively disables aggregation entirely, passing through all alerts as-is. This is unlikely to be what you want, unless you have a very low alert volume or your upstream notification system performs its own grouping.

group_interval.description: How long to wait before sending a notification about new alerts that are added to a group of alerts for which an initial notification has already been sent. (Usually ~5m or more.)

group_wait.description: How long to initially wait to send a notification for a group of alerts. Allows to wait for an inhibiting alert to arrive or collect more initial alerts for the same group. (Usually ~0s to few minutes.)

receiver.description: The name of the receiver to route the alerts to.

repeat_interval.description: How long to wait before sending a notification again if it has already been sent successfully for an alert. (Usually ~3h or more).

continue.description: Whether an alert should continue matching subsequent sibling nodes.

group_by.description: The labels by which incoming alerts are grouped together. For example, multiple alerts coming in for cluster=A and alertname=LatencyHigh would be batched into a single group. To aggregate by all possible labels use the special value '...' as the sole label name, for example: group_by: ['...']. This effectively disables aggregation entirely, passing through all alerts as-is. This is unlikely to be what you want, unless you have a very low alert volume or your upstream notification system performs its own grouping.

group_interval.description: How long to wait before sending a notification about new alerts that are added to a group of alerts for which an initial notification has already been sent. (Usually ~5m or more.)

group_wait.description: How long to initially wait to send a notification for a group of alerts. Allows to wait for an inhibiting alert to arrive or collect more initial alerts for the same group. (Usually ~0s to few minutes.)

match.description: A set of equality matchers an alert has to fulfill to match the node. This field is deprecated and will be removed after 10th March 2026, use `matchers` in the `routes` instead

match_regex.description: A set of regex-matchers an alert has to fulfill to match the node. This field is deprecated and will be removed after 10th March 2026, use `matchers` in the `routes` instead

matchers.description: A list of matchers that an alert has to fulfill to match the node. A matcher is a string with a syntax inspired by PromQL and OpenMetrics.

receiver.description: The name of the receiver to route the alerts to.

repeat_interval.description: How long to wait before sending a notification again if it has already been sent successfully for an alert. (Usually ~3h or more).

grafana_admin_enabled.description: If true, a default Grafana server admin user is created. It's recommended to set this to false and use STACKIT SSO (Owner or Observability Grafana Server Admin role) instead. It is still possible to manually create a new Grafana admin user via the Grafana UI later.

grafana_initial_admin_password.description: Specifies an initial Grafana admin password.

grafana_initial_admin_user.description: Specifies an initial Grafana admin username.

grafana_public_read_access.description: If true, anyone can access Grafana dashboards without logging in.

metrics_retention_days.description: Specifies for how many days the raw metrics are kept. Default is set to `90`.

metrics_retention_days_1h_downsampling.description: Specifies for how many days the 1h downsampled metrics are kept. must be less than the value of the 5m downsampling retention. Default is set to `90`.

metrics_retention_days_5m_downsampling.description: Specifies for how many days the 5m downsampled metrics are kept. must be less than the value of the general retention. Default is set to `90`.

block.description:
Associates an existing public IP to a network interface. This is useful for situations where you have a pre-allocated public IP or unable to use the `stackit_public_ip` resource to create a new public IP. Must have a `region` specified in the provider configuration.

!> The `stackit_public_ip_associate` resource should not be used together with the `stackit_public_ip` resource for the same public IP or for the same network interface. 
Using both resources together for the same public IP or network interface WILL lead to conflicts, as they both have control of the public IP and network interface association.

zones.description: Specify a list of domain filters for externalDNS (e.g., `foo.runs.onstackit.cloud`)

enabled.description: Flag to enable/disable Observability extensions.

instance_id.description: Observability instance ID to choose which Observability instance is used. Required when enabled is set to `true`.

observability.description: A single observability block as defined below.

extensions.description: A single extensions block as defined below.

end.description: End time of hibernation in crontab syntax. E.g. `0 8 * * *` for waking up the cluster at 8am.

start.description: Start time of cluster hibernation in crontab syntax. E.g. `0 18 * * *` for starting everyday at 6pm.

timezone.description: Timezone name corresponding to a file in the IANA Time Zone database. i.e. `Europe/Berlin`.

hibernations.description: One or more hibernation block as defined below.

id.description: Terraform's internal resource ID. It is structured as "`project_id`,`region`,`name`".

kubernetes_version_min.description: The minimum Kubernetes version. This field will be used to set the minimum kubernetes version on creation/update of the cluster. If unset, the latest supported Kubernetes version will be used. SKE automatically updates the cluster Kubernetes version if you have set `maintenance.enable_kubernetes_version_updates` to true or if there is a mandatory update, as described in [General information for Kubernetes & OS updates](https://docs.stackit.cloud/products/runtime/kubernetes-engine/basics/version-updates/). To get the current kubernetes version being used for your cluster, use the read-only `kubernetes_version_used` field.

kubernetes_version_used.description: Full Kubernetes version used. For example, if 1.22 was set in `kubernetes_version_min`, this value may result to 1.22.15. SKE automatically updates the cluster Kubernetes version if you have set `maintenance.enable_kubernetes_version_updates` to true or if there is a mandatory update, as described in [General information for Kubernetes & OS updates](https://docs.stackit.cloud/products/runtime/kubernetes-engine/basics/version-updates/).

enable_kubernetes_version_updates.description: Flag to enable/disable auto-updates of the Kubernetes version. Defaults to `true`. SKE automatically updates the cluster Kubernetes version if you have set `maintenance.enable_kubernetes_version_updates` to true or if there is a mandatory update, as described in [General information for Kubernetes & OS updates](https://docs.stackit.cloud/products/runtime/kubernetes-engine/basics/version-updates/).

enable_machine_image_version_updates.description: Flag to enable/disable auto-updates of the OS image version. Defaults to `true`. SKE automatically updates the cluster Kubernetes version if you have set `maintenance.enable_kubernetes_version_updates` to true or if there is a mandatory update, as described in [General information for Kubernetes & OS updates](https://docs.stackit.cloud/products/runtime/kubernetes-engine/basics/version-updates/).

end.description: Time for maintenance window end. E.g. `01:23:45Z`, `05:00:00+02:00`.

start.description: Time for maintenance window start. E.g. `01:23:45Z`, `05:00:00+02:00`.

maintenance.description: A single maintenance block as defined below.

name.description: The cluster name.

access_scope.description: Access scope of the control plane. It defines if the Kubernetes control plane is public or only available inside a STACKIT Network Area.Possible values are: `PUBLIC`, `SNA`. The field is immutable!

id.description: ID of the STACKIT Network Area (SNA) network into which the cluster will be deployed.

allow_system_components.description: Allow system components to run on this node pool.

availability_zones.description: Specify a list of availability zones. E.g. `eu01-m`

cri.description: Specifies the container runtime. Defaults to `containerd`

labels.description: Labels to add to each node.

max_surge.description: Maximum number of additional VMs that are created during an update. If set (larger than 0), then it must be at least the amount of zones configured for the nodepool. The `max_surge` and `max_unavailable` fields cannot both be unset at the same time.

max_unavailable.description: Maximum number of VMs that that can be unavailable during an update. If set (larger than 0), then it must be at least the amount of zones configured for the nodepool. The `max_surge` and `max_unavailable` fields cannot both be unset at the same time.

maximum.description: Maximum number of nodes in the pool.

minimum.description: Minimum number of nodes in the pool.

name.description: Specifies the name of the node pool.

os_name.description: The name of the OS image. Defaults to `flatcar`.

os_version.description: This field is deprecated, use `os_version_min` to configure the version and `os_version_used` to get the currently used version instead.

os_version_min.description: The minimum OS image version. This field will be used to set the minimum OS image version on creation/update of the cluster. If unset, the latest supported OS image version will be used. SKE automatically updates the cluster Kubernetes version if you have set `maintenance.enable_kubernetes_version_updates` to true or if there is a mandatory update, as described in [General information for Kubernetes & OS updates](https://docs.stackit.cloud/products/runtime/kubernetes-engine/basics/version-updates/). To get the current OS image version being used for the node pool, use the read-only `os_version_used` field.

os_version_used.description: Full OS image version used. For example, if 3815.2 was set in `os_version_min`, this value may result to 3815.2.2. SKE automatically updates the cluster Kubernetes version if you have set `maintenance.enable_kubernetes_version_updates` to true or if there is a mandatory update, as described in [General information for Kubernetes & OS updates](https://docs.stackit.cloud/products/runtime/kubernetes-engine/basics/version-updates/).

key.description: Taint key to be applied to a node.

value.description: Taint value corresponding to the taint key.

taints.description: Specifies a taint list as defined below.

volume_size.description: The volume size in GB. Defaults to `20`

volume_type.description: Specifies the volume type. Defaults to `storage_premium_perf1`.

node_pools.description:
One or more `node_pool` block as defined below.
To keep your Terraform plans clean and readable, always append new node pools to the end of the list.

pod_address_ranges.description: The network ranges (in CIDR notation) used by pods of the cluster.

project_id.description: STACKIT project ID to which the cluster is associated.

region.description: The resource region. If not defined, the provider region is used.

SKE Cluster Resource schema. Must have a `region` specified in the provider configuration.

-> When updating `node_pools` of a `stackit_ske_cluster`, the Terraform plan might appear incorrect as it matches the node pools by index rather than by name. However, the SKE API correctly identifies node pools by name and applies the intended changes. Please review your changes carefully to ensure the correct configuration will be applied.

expiration.description: Expiration time of the kubeconfig, in seconds. Defaults to `3600`

refresh.description: If set to true, the provider will check if the kubeconfig has expired and will generated a new valid one in-place

refresh_before.description: Number of seconds before expiration to trigger refresh of the kubeconfig at. Only used if refresh is set to true.

region.description: The resource region. If not defined, the provider region is used.

instance_id.description: ID of the SQLServer Flex instance.

project_id.description: STACKIT project ID to which the instance is associated.

roles.description: Database access levels for the user. The values for the default roles are: `##STACKIT_DatabaseManager##`, `##STACKIT_LoginManager##`, `##STACKIT_ProcessManager##`, `##STACKIT_ServerManager##`, `##STACKIT_SQLAgentManager##`, `##STACKIT_SQLAgentUser##`

username.description: Username of the SQLServer Flex instance.

block.description: SQLServer Flex user resource schema. Must have a `region` specified in the provider configuration.

availability_zone.description: The availability zone of the volume.

description.description: The description of the volume.

encrypted.description: Indicates if the volume is encrypted.

kek_key_id.description: UUID of the key within the STACKIT-KMS to use for the encryption.

kek_key_version.description: Version of the key within the STACKIT-KMS to use for the encryption.

kek_keyring_id.description: UUID of the keyring where the key is located within the STACKTI-KMS.

key_payload_base64.description: Optional predefined secret, which will be encrypted against the key-encryption-key within the STACKIT-KMS. If not defined, a random secret will be generated by the API and encrypted against the STACKIT-KMS. If a key-payload is provided here, it must be base64 encoded.

key_payload_base64_wo.description: Optional predefined secret, which will be encrypted against the key-encryption-key within the STACKIT-KMS. If not defined, a random secret will be generated by the API and encrypted against the STACKIT-KMS. If a key-payload is provided here, it must be base64 encoded.

key_payload_base64_wo_version.description: Used together with `key_payload_base64_wo` to trigger an re-create. Increment this value when an update to `key_payload_base64_wo` is required.

service_account.description: Service-Account linked to the Key within the STACKIT-KMS.

encryption_parameters.description: Parameter to connect to a key-encryption-key within the STACKIT-KMS to create encrypted volumes. These parameters never leave the backend again. So these parameters are not present on imports or in the datasource. They live only in your Terraform state after creation of the resource.

id.description: Terraform's internal resource ID. It is structured as "`project_id`,`region`,`volume_id`".

labels.description: Labels are key-value string pairs which can be attached to a resource container

name.description: The name of the volume.

performance_class.description: The performance class of the volume. Possible values are documented in [Service plans BlockStorage](https://docs.stackit.cloud/products/storage/block-storage/basics/service-plans/#currently-available-service-plans-performance-classes)

project_id.description: STACKIT project ID to which the volume is associated.

region.description: The resource region. If not defined, the provider region is used.

server_id.description: The server ID of the server to which the volume is attached to.

size.description: The size of the volume in GB. It can only be updated to a larger value than the current size. Either `size` or `source` must be provided

id.description: The ID of the source, e.g. image ID

type.description: The type of the source. Possible values are: `volume`, `image`, `snapshot`, `backup`.

source.description: The source of the volume. It can be either a volume, an image, a snapshot or a backup. Either `size` or `source` must be provided

volume_id.description: The volume ID.

disable_target_security_group_assignment.description: Disable target security group assignemt to allow targets outside of the given network. Connectivity to targets need to be ensured by the customer, including routing and Security Groups (targetSecurityGroup can be assigned). Not changeable after creation.

description.description: The error description contains additional helpful user information to fix the error state of the Application Load Balancer. For example the IP 45.135.247.139 does not exist in the project, then the description will report: Floating IP "45.135.247.139" could not be found.

type.description: The error type specifies which part of the Application Load Balancer encountered the error. I.e. the API will not check if a provided public IP is actually available in the project. Instead the Application Load Balancer with try to use the provided IP and if not available reports TYPE_FIP_NOT_CONFIGURED error. Possible values are: `TYPE_UNSPECIFIED`, `TYPE_INTERNAL`, `TYPE_QUOTA_SECGROUP_EXCEEDED`, `TYPE_QUOTA_SECGROUPRULE_EXCEEDED`, `TYPE_PORT_NOT_CONFIGURED`, `TYPE_FIP_NOT_CONFIGURED`, `TYPE_TARGET_NOT_ACTIVE`, `TYPE_METRICS_MISCONFIGURED`, `TYPE_LOGS_MISCONFIGURED`.

errors.description: Reports all errors a Application Load Balancer has.

external_address.description: The external IP address where this Application Load Balancer is exposed. Not changeable after creation.

id.description: Terraform's internal resource ID. It is structured as `project_id`,`region`,`name`.

labels.description: Labels represent user-defined metadata as key-value pairs. Label count cannot exceed 64 per ALB.

host.description: Hostname to match. Supports wildcards (e.g. *.example.com).

name.description: The name of the cookie to use.

ttl.description: TTL specifies the time-to-live for the cookie. The default value is 0s, and it acts as a session cookie, expiring when the client session ends.

exact_match.description: Exact match for the header value.

name.description: Header name.

exact_match.description: Exact path match. Only a request path exactly equal to the value will match, e.g. '/foo' matches only '/foo', not '/foo/bar' or '/foobar'.

prefix.description: Prefix path match. Only matches on full segment boundaries, e.g. '/foo' matches '/foo' and '/foo/bar' but NOT '/foobar'.

path.description: Routing via path.

exact_match.description: Exact match for the query parameters value.

name.description: Query parameter name.

target_pool.description: Reference target pool by target pool name.

web_socket.description: If enabled, when client sends an HTTP request with and Upgrade header, indicating the desire to establish a Websocket connection, if backend server supports WebSocket, it responds with HTTP 101 status code, switching protocols from HTTP to WebSocket. Hence the client and the server can exchange data in real-time using one long-lived TCP connection.

rules.description: Routing rules under the specified host, matched by path prefix.

hosts.description: Defines routing rules grouped by hostname.

https.description: Configuration for handling HTTPS traffic on this listener.

name.description: Unique name for the listener

port.description: Port number on which the listener receives incoming traffic.

protocol.description: Protocol is the highest network protocol we understand to load balance. Possible values are: `PROTOCOL_UNSPECIFIED`, `PROTOCOL_HTTP`, `PROTOCOL_HTTPS`.

waf_config_name.description: Enable Web Application Firewall (WAF), referenced by name. See "Application Load Balancer - Web Application Firewall API" for more information.

listeners.description: List of all listeners which will accept traffic. Limited to 20.

id.description: ID of the security Group

name.description: Name of the security Group

load_balancer_security_group.description: Security Group permitting network traffic from the LoadBalancer to the targets. Useful when disableTargetSecurityGroupAssignment=true to manually assign target security groups to targets.

name.description: Application Load balancer name.

network_id.description: STACKIT network ID the Application Load Balancer and/or targets are in.

role.description: The role defines how the Application Load Balancer is using the network. Possible values are: `ROLE_UNSPECIFIED`, `ROLE_LISTENERS_AND_TARGETS`, `ROLE_LISTENERS`, `ROLE_TARGETS`.

networks.description: List of networks that listeners and targets reside in.

allowed_source_ranges.description: Application Load Balancer is accessible only from an IP address in this range.

access_control.description: Use this option to limit the IP ranges that can use the Application Load Balancer.

ephemeral_address.description: This option automates the handling of the external IP address for an Application Load Balancer. If set to true a new IP address will be automatically created. It will also be automatically deleted when the Load Balancer is deleted.

credentials_ref.description: Credentials reference for logging. This reference is created via the observability create endpoint and the credential needs to contain the basic auth username and password for the logging solution the push URL points to. Then this enables monitoring via remote write for the Application Load Balancer.

push_url.description: Credentials reference for logging. This reference is created via the observability create endpoint and the credential needs to contain the basic auth username and password for the logging solution the push URL points to. Then this enables monitoring via remote write for the Application Load Balancer.

credentials_ref.description: Credentials reference for metrics. This reference is created via the observability create endpoint and the credential needs to contain the basic auth username and password for the metrics solution the push URL points to. Then this enables monitoring via remote write for the Application Load Balancer.

push_url.description: Credentials reference for metrics. This reference is created via the observability create endpoint and the credential needs to contain the basic auth username and password for the metrics solution the push URL points to. Then this enables monitoring via remote write for the Application Load Balancer.

observability.description: We offer Load Balancer observability via STACKIT Observability or external solutions.

private_network_only.description: Application Load Balancer is accessible only via a private network ip address. Not changeable after creation.

options.description: Defines any optional functionality you want to have enabled on your Application Load Balancer.

plan_id.description: Service Plan configures the size of the Application Load Balancer.

project_id.description: STACKIT project ID to which the Application Load Balancer is associated.

region.description: The resource region. If not defined, the provider region is used.

healthy_threshold.description: Healthy threshold of the health checking.

ok_status.description: List of HTTP status codes that indicate a healthy response.

path.description: Path to send the health check request to.

http_health_checks.description: Options for the HTTP health checking.

interval.description: Interval duration of health checking in seconds.

interval_jitter.description: Interval duration threshold of the health checking in seconds.

timeout.description: Active health checking timeout duration in seconds.

unhealthy_threshold.description: Unhealthy threshold of the health checking.

name.description: Target pool name.

target_port.description: The number identifying the port where each target listens for traffic.

ip.description: Private target IP, which must by unique within a target pool.

targets.description: List of all targets which will be used in the pool. Limited to 250.

custom_ca.description: Specifies a custom Certificate Authority (CA). When provided, the target pool will trust certificates signed by this CA, in addition to any system-trusted CAs. This is useful for scenarios where the target pool needs to communicate with servers using self-signed or internally-issued certificates. Enabled needs to be set to true and skip validation to false for this option.

enabled.description: Enable TLS (Transport Layer Security) bridging for the connection between Application Load Balancer and targets in this pool. When enabled, public CAs are trusted. Can be used in tandem with the options either custom CA or skip validation or alone.

skip_certificate_validation.description: Bypass certificate validation for TLS bridging in this target pool. This option is insecure and can only be used with public CAs by setting enabled true. Meant to be used for testing purposes only!

target_pools.description: List of all target pools which will be used in the Application Load Balancer. Limited to 20.

id.description: ID of the security Group

name.description: Name of the security Group

target_security_group.description: Security Group that allows the targets to receive traffic from the LoadBalancer. Useful when disableTargetSecurityGroupAssignment=true to manually assign target security groups to targets.

version.description: Application Load Balancer resource version. Used for concurrency safe updates.

value_match_condition.description: Defines how multiple matchers within this rule are combined (ALL, ANY, NONE). Defaults to ANY.

values.description: A list of glob patterns to match against the request path. At least one value is required. Examples: "/shop/*" or "*/img/*"

block.description:
Image datasource schema. Must have a `region` specified in the provider configuration.

~> Important: When using the `name`, `name_regex`, or `filter` attributes to select images dynamically, be aware that image IDs may change frequently. Each OS patch or update results in a new unique image ID. If this data source is used to populate fields like `boot_volume.source_id` in a server resource, it may cause Terraform to detect changes and recreate the associated resource.

To avoid unintended updates or resource replacements:
 - Prefer using a static `image_id` to pin a specific image version.
 - If you accept automatic image updates but wish to suppress resource changes, use a `lifecycle` block to ignore relevant changes. For example:

```hcl
resource "stackit_server" "example" {
  boot_volume = {
    size        = 64
    source_type = "image"
    source_id   = data.stackit_image.latest.id
  }

  lifecycle {
    ignore_changes = [boot_volume[0].source_id]
  }
}
```

You can also list available images using the [STACKIT CLI](https://github.com/stackitcloud/stackit-cli):

```bash
stackit image list
```

disable_security_group_assignment.description: If set to true, this will disable the automatic assignment of a security group to the load balancer's targets. This option is primarily used to allow targets that are not within the load balancer's own network or SNA (STACKIT Network area). When this is enabled, you are fully responsible for ensuring network connectivity to the targets, including managing all routing and security group rules manually. This setting cannot be changed after the load balancer is created.

security_group_id.description: The ID of the egress security group assigned to the Load Balancer's internal machines. This ID is essential for allowing traffic from the Load Balancer to targets in different networks or STACKIT Network areas (SNA). To enable this, create a security group rule for your target VMs and set the `remote_security_group_id` of that rule to this value. This is typically used when `disable_security_group_assignment` is set to `true`.

Expr-lang filter for filtering machine types.

Examples:
- vcpus == 2
- ram >= 2048
- extraSpecs.cpu == "intel-icelake-generic"
- extraSpecs.cpu == "intel-icelake-generic" && vcpus == 2

Syntax reference: https://expr-lang.org/docs/language-definition

You can also list available machine-types using the [STACKIT CLI](https://github.com/stackitcloud/stackit-cli):

```bash
stackit server machine-type list
```

interval.description: Specifies the frequency at which rules within the group are evaluated. The interval must be at least 60 seconds and defaults to 60 seconds if not set. Supported formats include hours, minutes, and seconds, either singly or in combination. Examples of valid formats are: '5h30m40s', '5h', '5h30m', '60m', and '60s'.

name.description: The name of the alert group. Is the identifier and must be unique in the group.

project_id.description: STACKIT project ID to which the alert group is associated.

alert.description: The name of the alert rule. Is the identifier and must be unique in the group.

annotations.description: A map of key:value. Annotations to add or overwrite for each alert

expression.description: The PromQL expression to evaluate. Every evaluation cycle this is evaluated at the current time, and all resultant time series become pending/firing alerts.

for.description: Alerts are considered firing once they have been returned for this long. Alerts which have not yet fired for long enough are considered pending. Default is 0s

labels.description: A map of key:value. Labels to add or overwrite for each alert

record.description: The name of the metric. It's the identifier and must be unique in the group.

block.description: Observability alert group datasource schema. Used to create alerts based on metrics (Thanos). Must have a `region` specified in the provider configuration.

acl.description: The access control list for this instance. Each entry is an IP address range that is permitted to access, in CIDR notation.

opsgenie_api_url.description: The host to send OpsGenie API requests to. Must be a valid URL

resolve_timeout.description: The default value used by alertmanager if the alert does not include EndsAt. After this time passes, it can declare the alert as resolved if it has not been updated. This has no impact on alerts from Prometheus, as they always include EndsAt.

smtp_auth_identity.description: SMTP authentication information. Must be a valid email address

smtp_auth_password.description: SMTP Auth using LOGIN and PLAIN.

smtp_auth_username.description: SMTP Auth using CRAM-MD5, LOGIN and PLAIN. If empty, Alertmanager doesn't authenticate to the SMTP server.

smtp_from.description: The default SMTP From header field. Must be a valid email address

smtp_smart_host.description: The default SMTP smarthost used for sending emails, including port number. Port number usually is 25, or 587 for SMTP over TLS (sometimes referred to as STARTTLS).

auth_identity.description: SMTP authentication information. Must be a valid email address

from.description: The sender email address. Must be a valid email address

send_resolved.description: Whether to notify about resolved alerts.

smart_host.description: The SMTP host through which emails are sent.

to.description: The email address to send notifications to. Must be a valid email address

name.description: Name of the receiver.

api_url.description: The host to send OpsGenie API requests to. Must be a valid URL

priority.description: Priority of the alert. Possible values are: `P1`, `P2`, `P3`, `P4`, `P5`.

send_resolved.description: Whether to notify about resolved alerts.

tags.description: Comma separated list of tags attached to the notifications.

google_chat.description: Google Chat webhooks require special handling, set this to true if the webhook is for Google Chat.

ms_teams.description: Microsoft Teams webhooks require special handling, set this to true if the webhook is for Microsoft Teams.

send_resolved.description: Whether to notify about resolved alerts.

url.description: The endpoint to send HTTP POST requests to. Must be a valid URL

continue.description: Whether an alert should continue matching subsequent sibling nodes.

group_by.description: The labels by which incoming alerts are grouped together. For example, multiple alerts coming in for cluster=A and alertname=LatencyHigh would be batched into a single group. To aggregate by all possible labels use the special value '...' as the sole label name, for example: group_by: ['...']. This effectively disables aggregation entirely, passing through all alerts as-is. This is unlikely to be what you want, unless you have a very low alert volume or your upstream notification system performs its own grouping.

group_interval.description: How long to wait before sending a notification about new alerts that are added to a group of alerts for which an initial notification has already been sent. (Usually ~5m or more.)

group_wait.description: How long to initially wait to send a notification for a group of alerts. Allows to wait for an inhibiting alert to arrive or collect more initial alerts for the same group. (Usually ~0s to few minutes.) .

receiver.description: The name of the receiver to route the alerts to.

repeat_interval.description: How long to wait before sending a notification again if it has already been sent successfully for an alert. (Usually ~3h or more).

continue.description: Whether an alert should continue matching subsequent sibling nodes.

group_by.description: The labels by which incoming alerts are grouped together. For example, multiple alerts coming in for cluster=A and alertname=LatencyHigh would be batched into a single group. To aggregate by all possible labels use the special value '...' as the sole label name, for example: group_by: ['...']. This effectively disables aggregation entirely, passing through all alerts as-is. This is unlikely to be what you want, unless you have a very low alert volume or your upstream notification system performs its own grouping.

group_interval.description: How long to wait before sending a notification about new alerts that are added to a group of alerts for which an initial notification has already been sent. (Usually ~5m or more.)

group_wait.description: How long to initially wait to send a notification for a group of alerts. Allows to wait for an inhibiting alert to arrive or collect more initial alerts for the same group. (Usually ~0s to few minutes.)

match.description: A set of equality matchers an alert has to fulfill to match the node. This field is deprecated and will be removed after 10th March 2026, use `matchers` in the `routes` instead

match_regex.description: A set of regex-matchers an alert has to fulfill to match the node. This field is deprecated and will be removed after 10th March 2026, use `matchers` in the `routes` instead

matchers.description: A list of matchers that an alert has to fulfill to match the node. A matcher is a string with a syntax inspired by PromQL and OpenMetrics.

receiver.description: The name of the receiver to route the alerts to.

repeat_interval.description: How long to wait before sending a notification again if it has already been sent successfully for an alert. (Usually ~3h or more).

grafana_admin_enabled.description: If true, a default Grafana server admin user was created.

grafana_initial_admin_password.description: Specifies an initial Grafana admin password.

grafana_initial_admin_user.description: Specifies an initial Grafana admin username.

grafana_public_read_access.description: If true, anyone can access Grafana dashboards without logging in.

is_updatable.description: Specifies if the instance can be updated.

logs_retention_days.description: Specifies for how many days the logs are kept. Default is set to `7`.

metrics_retention_days.description: Specifies for how many days the raw metrics are kept. Default is set to `90`.

metrics_retention_days_1h_downsampling.description: Specifies for how many days the 1h downsampled metrics are kept. must be less than the value of the 5m downsampling retention. Default is set to `90`.

metrics_retention_days_5m_downsampling.description: Specifies for how many days the 5m downsampled metrics are kept. must be less than the value of the general retention. Default is set to `90`.

name.description: The name of the Observability instance.

project_id.description: STACKIT project ID to which the instance is associated.

traces_retention_days.description: Specifies for how many days the traces are kept. Default is set to `7`.

kubernetes_version_min.description: The minimum Kubernetes version, this field is always nil. SKE automatically updates the cluster Kubernetes version if you have set `maintenance.enable_kubernetes_version_updates` to true or if there is a mandatory update, as described in [General information for Kubernetes & OS updates](https://docs.stackit.cloud/products/runtime/kubernetes-engine/basics/version-updates/). To get the current kubernetes version being used for your cluster, use the `kubernetes_version_used` field.

To get the current OS image version being used for the node pool, use the read-only `os_version_used` field.

backend.description: The remote backend to use, e.g. `remote` or `http`.

The configuration of the remote backend. Although this is optional, most backends require some configuration.

The object can use any arguments that would be valid in the equivalent `terraform { backend "<TYPE>" { ... } }` block.

decode_tfvars.description: A rarely-needed function which takes a string containing the content of a `.tfvars` file and returns an object describing the raw variable values it defines.
decode_tfvars.summary: Parse a string containing syntax like that used in a `.tfvars` file

encode_expr.description: A rarely-needed function which takes any value and produces a string containing Terraform language expression syntax approximating that value.
encode_expr.summary: Produce a string representation of an arbitrary value using Terraform expression syntax

encode_tfvars.description: A rarely-needed function which takes an object value and produces a string containing a description of that object using the same syntax as Terraform CLI would expect in a `.tfvars`.
encode_tfvars.summary: Produce a string representation of an object using the same syntax as for `.tfvars` files
